discharged_obligations.PO-BH-2: DERIVED_CONDITIONAL: Hopf phase closure gives positive integer admissibility
discharged_obligations.PO-BH-3: DERIVED_CONDITIONAL: boundary involution gives minimal balanced orientation sector d=2
discharged_obligations.PO-BH-4: DERIVED_CONDITIONAL: minimal non-involutive cyclic sector gives d=3
discharged_obligations.PO-BH-8: DERIVED_CONDITIONAL: primitive low-energy closure selectors give {1,2,3}

PO-BH-2.remaining_blocker: This gives integer admissibility but not the full primitive spectrum by itself.
PO-BH-2.statement: Hopf fiber identification psi~psi+2*pi and Phi_d=exp(i d psi)Phi_0 imply exp(i2*pi*d)=1, so positive closure dimensions are positive integers.
PO-BH-2.target: derive Hopf phase closure from global single-valuedness

PO-BH-3.remaining_blocker: This gives the minimal orientation pair but not the full weak gauge group.
PO-BH-3.statement: A boundary involution Iota^2=id has eigenvalues lambda=+/-1; the minimal balanced nontrivial representation contains both signs and has dimension 2.
PO-BH-3.target: derive Z2 orientation involution from boundary orientation reversal

PO-BH-4.remaining_blocker: This gives the minimal non-involutive cyclic order but not the full color gauge group.
PO-BH-4.statement: A cyclic sector has g^n=id; order 1 is identity and order 2 is the involution, so the first non-involutive cyclic order is 3.
PO-BH-4.target: derive minimal cyclic channel beyond identity and involution

PO-BH-8.remaining_blocker: Higher closures are not impossible; they remain higher/composite/excess candidates outside this primitive layer.
PO-BH-8.statement: Identity/reference, minimal orientation pair, and minimal non-involutive cyclic channel give primitive selectors 1, 2, and 3.

negative_results: replacement claim is not ready because downstream finite algebra, charge, anomaly, gauge, mass, and dynamics derivations remain open
higher closures are not impossible; they are outside the primitive identity/orientation/minimal-cyclic layer

mission remains full Standard Model derivation from BHSM
